mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 5.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 60 percent of instances where UNRATE falls below 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer credit balances combined with falling housing starts indicate a credit-driven consumption boom that offsets real estate weakness, leading to positive equity returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: Joint signal produces negative S&P 500 60-day forward returns in more than 50 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated inflation, rising industrial production signals supply-side resilience that mitigates stagflation fears, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are significantly lower when INDPRO rises during high inflation periods compared to baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly increases in real GDP reflect sustained economic growth that directly lifts corporate profit expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no statistical difference between GDPC1 growth deciles.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5 percent, the cost of capital rises sufficiently to compress equity multiples, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 60 percent of instances where FEDFUNDS crosses above 4.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total employment and falling unemployment indicates broad-based hiring that sustains wage growth without triggering immediate inflation fears, boosting S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS and UNRATE signal fails to predict S&P 500 20-day forward returns with a Rank IC above 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: In a housing downturn, rising consumer credit balances signal a shift to financial asset demand that cushions equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative when HOUST falls and RSAFS rises simultaneously.
